creator: girgen@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Cullum Smith from comment #20)
Hi!

I'm just truggling a bit doing the right thing here. It seems that since we have Heimdal in base, using the patch will catch the base heimdal still fail even if I config the port to use the more updated package.

checking for library containing gss_store_cred_into... no
configure: error: could not find function 'gss_store_cred_into' required for GSSAPI
===>  Script "configure" failed unexpectedly.


===> The following configuration options are available for postgresql16-server-16.7:
     DEBUG=off: Build with debugging support
     DOCS=on: Build and/or install documentation
     DTRACE=off: Build with DTrace probes
     GSSAPI=on: GSSAPI Security API support
     LDAP=off: Build with LDAP authentication support
     LLVM=on: Build with support for JIT-compiling expressions
     LZ4=on: Build with LZ4 compression support
     NLS=off: Use internationalized messages
     OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS=on: Builds with compiler optimizations (-O3)
     PAM=off: Build with PAM Support
     SSL=on: Build with OpenSSL support
     TZDATA=on: Use internal timezone database
     XML=on: Build with XML data type
     ZSTD=on: Build with ZSTD compression support
====> GSSAPI Security API support: you have to select exactly one of them
     GSSAPI_NONE=off: Build without GSSAPI support
     GSSAPI_BASE=off: Use GSSAPI from base
     GSSAPI_HEIMDAL=on: Use Heimdal GSSAPI from security/heimdal
     GSSAPI_MIT=off: Use MIT GSSAPI from security/krb5




So, is your suggestion not to support heiumdal at all? Or do I need to build a version of the base system without heimdal to even get that option to build? Or can I pin it to the package version?

Please advice.

Palle
